edges.description: Active Gq/11 stimulates PLC-beta, generating DAG to activate PKC
ERK activation drives cell cycle progression and proliferation

nodes.description: GNAQ and GNA11 encode alpha subunits of heterotrimeric Gq/11 G-proteins. Mutations at Q209 (most common) or R183 lock the protein in a constitutively active GTP-bound state, resulting in persistent downstream signaling through phospholipase C beta, protein kinase C, and ultimately the MAPK pathway. These mutations are mutually exclusive and early, initiating events.

evidence.reference_title: [Uveal melanoma: current insights into clinical relevance of genetic testing].
evidence.snippet: Recently, major key genes involved in uveal melanoma development, GNAQ, GNA11, BAP1, SF3B1 and EIF1AX, have been identified.

nodes.description: Constitutively active GNAQ/GNA11 signaling activates phospholipase C-beta, generating diacylglycerol (DAG) that activates protein kinase C (PKC). PKC in turn activates the RAF-MEK-ERK cascade, driving melanocyte proliferation. This pathway represents a therapeutic target with MEK and PKC inhibitors.
Persistent MAPK signaling drives proliferation of uveal melanocytes, leading to tumor formation. Unlike cutaneous melanoma, uveal melanoma rarely harbors BRAF or NRAS mutations.
